# Story 1.5: Settings, Feedback, and Account Entry Points

Status: done

<!-- Generated by BMAD 6.8 create-story workflow on 2026-06-19. -->

## Story

As a traveler,
I want settings for key ownership, account data, and feedback,
so that I can trust and control my use of Nomad.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given the authenticated user opens Settings, when the screen renders, then it shows login state, BYOK status, account deletion, data export, and feedback entry points.
2. Given BYOK is not configured, when the user reaches AI quota education moments, then the app can route to BYOK education and configuration without blocking early platform-quota use.
3. Given the user requests export or deletion, when the action is confirmed, then backend account queues are used and the UI displays queued/in-progress/done states.
4. Given feedback opens in WebView, when WebView fails or the site blocks embedding, then the app falls back to the system browser and emits `feedback_open`, `feedback_submit_success`, and `feedback_submit_fail` telemetry where available.
5. Given Settings/BYOK/account/feedback contracts are used, when implementation is complete, then `docs/api/openapi.yaml` remains the API source of truth and `packages/types/src/api-types.ts` is regenerated rather than hand-edited.
6. Given Settings analytics are emitted, when events are tracked, then payloads use semantic ids and do not include raw API keys, BYOK secret material, phone numbers, cookies, OTPs, session ids, feedback text, or URLs.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Align Settings, BYOK, account, and feedback API contracts (AC: 1, 3, 4, 5, 6)
  - [x] Add or update OpenAPI schemas for `UserKeyStatusResponse`, `ByokValidateRequest`, `ByokValidateResponse`, `ByokSaveRequest`, `AccountTaskResponse`, and `FeedbackLinkResponse`.
  - [x] Add OpenAPI paths for `POST /account/export` and `DELETE /account` returning queued account task state.
  - [x] Align BYOK paths so generated mobile types can use `GET/POST/DELETE /user-key`, with `/byok/validate` and `/byok/save` preserved as compatibility helpers.
  - [x] Keep all BYOK request bodies out of analytics and logs; responses may return only status and key references, never plaintext keys.
  - [x] Run `pnpm -F nomad-types run generate` after OpenAPI edits.
- [x] Build server-side Settings support without duplicating account/BYOK stacks (AC: 1, 3, 4, 5)
  - [x] Extend existing `apps/server/src/routes/byok.ts` rather than adding a second BYOK module.
  - [x] Reuse existing `apps/server/src/routes/account.ts` queue wiring; account export and delete must call `app.queues.exportQueue.add` and `app.queues.deleteQueue.add`.
  - [x] Add a focused `apps/server/src/routes/feedback.ts` for `GET /feedback/link`, returning the official `https://support.qq.com/product/{PRODUCT_ID}` URL with optional safe source context.
  - [x] Register new feedback routes from `apps/server/src/index.ts` using NodeNext-compatible `.js` import specifiers.
  - [x] Use existing `authGuard` and standard `reply.sendError` envelopes for protected routes and invalid BYOK bodies.
- [x] Implement mobile Settings entry and Settings screen (AC: 1, 2, 3, 4, 6)
  - [x] Update `apps/mobile/src/App.tsx` to route authenticated users between Home, Settings, and existing Planner handoff placeholder without bypassing `/me`.
  - [x] Update `apps/mobile/src/home/HomeScreen.tsx` so the menu button opens Settings instead of being inert.
  - [x] Add a Settings feature folder such as `apps/mobile/src/settings/` with API client, screen component, and focused tests.
  - [x] Render Settings as a compact mobile product surface with groups for account/login, AI/BYOK, data/privacy, and feedback; keep touch targets >=44px and card radii <=8px.
  - [x] Show platform quota copy when BYOK is not configured and provide a BYOK configuration path without blocking the rest of the app.
  - [x] Support BYOK validate/save/delete states and user-facing copy from UX: "已保存", "密钥无效，请检查", and "已回退到平台额度".
  - [x] Support data export and account deletion confirmation; show returned task status copy for queued/in-progress/done states.
  - [x] Support feedback open through returned official URL and fallback to system browser/fallback form copy if embedding/open fails.
- [x] Add privacy-safe Settings analytics (AC: 4, 6)
  - [x] Extend the existing analytics wrapper in `apps/mobile/src/auth/analytics.ts`; do not scatter provider-specific calls in Settings components.
  - [x] Emit semantic events for Settings view, BYOK save/delete outcomes, account export/delete actions, and feedback open/submit success/fail.
  - [x] Ensure sanitizer drops API key aliases, BYOK secret fields, feedback text/body, URLs, phone/session/cookie/OTP fields, and candidate/internal scores already covered by previous stories.
- [x] Add focused automated coverage and probes (AC: 1-6)
  - [x] Add server probe or tests for BYOK status/validate/save/delete, account export/delete queue use, feedback link generation, auth scoping, and no plaintext key response.
  - [x] Add mobile tests for Settings entry from Home, login state rendering, BYOK save/failure/delete, account task statuses, feedback fallback, and privacy-safe analytics.
  - [x] Ensure tests fail before implementation during the red phase, then pass after implementation.
  - [x] Run `pnpm -F nomad-mobile test`, `pnpm -F nomad-mobile build`, `pnpm -F nomad-server exec tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit`, `pnpm -F nomad-types run generate`, `pnpm ci:auth`, `pnpm -F nomad-server run test:settings`, and `pnpm -r build`.

### Review Findings

- [x] [Review][Patch] BYOK save discarded the sealed payload and used a non-unique key reference [apps/server/src/routes/byok.ts]
- [x] [Review][Patch] BYOK validate/save contract and server validation were misaligned for invalid keys and nullable provider [docs/api/openapi.yaml, apps/server/src/routes/byok.ts]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Feedback fallback reported success without a real external submission path [apps/mobile/src/settings/SettingsScreen.tsx]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Feedback open failure copy implied a browser opened when no URL was opened [apps/mobile/src/settings/SettingsScreen.tsx]
- [x] [Review][Patch] BYOK save errors collapsed operational failures into invalid-key copy [apps/mobile/src/settings/SettingsScreen.tsx]
- [x] [Review][Patch] Account export queued immediately without confirmation and account actions could be double-submitted [apps/mobile/src/settings/SettingsScreen.tsx]
- [x] [Review][Patch] AI quota education moments did not provide a direct route to BYOK configuration [apps/mobile/src/home/HomeScreen.tsx]
- [x] [Review][Patch] BYOK status load failure could show optimistic platform-quota copy [apps/mobile/src/settings/SettingsScreen.tsx]

## Dev Notes

### Current State

- Story 1.4 is complete on `main` and introduced authenticated Home/Library UI, `HomeScreen`, `HomeApiClient`, Settings-ready App routing shape, and privacy-safe analytics patterns.
- `apps/mobile/src/App.tsx` currently restores `/me`, renders `HomeScreen`, and displays a Planner handoff placeholder. Story 1.5 should add a Settings view state rather than replacing auth restore or Planner handoff behavior.
- `apps/mobile/src/home/HomeScreen.tsx` has a menu icon button that is currently a placeholder. Use it as the Settings entry point.
- `apps/mobile/src/auth/api.ts` and `apps/mobile/src/home/api.ts` define the browser fetch/client pattern: `VITE_API_BASE_URL` fallback, `credentials: "include"`, standard error normalization, and generated OpenAPI types from `nomad-types`.
- `apps/mobile/src/auth/analytics.ts` is the shared analytics wrapper. Extend event names and sanitizer there.
- `apps/server/src/routes/byok.ts` exists but currently exposes `/byok/set` and `DELETE /byok`; OpenAPI already describes `/user-key`, `/byok/validate`, and `/byok/save`. Align existing routes instead of creating a second BYOK stack.
- `apps/server/src/routes/account.ts` exists and queues export/delete tasks through `app.queues`. OpenAPI does not yet document these account queue endpoints.
- `docs/api/openapi.yaml` already has `FeedbackLinkResponse` and `/feedback/link`, but there is no registered server route yet.

### API Contract Guardrails

- `docs/api/openapi.yaml` is the API source of truth; regenerate `packages/types/src/api-types.ts` after contract edits and never hand-edit generated types.
- Protected Settings routes must use `authGuard` and must scope state to `req.user!.id`.
- Standard error responses must use `reply.sendError` from `apps/server/src/plugins/error-envelope.ts`.
- BYOK plaintext key material may appear only in incoming request bodies long enough to validate/seal it. Do not return it, log it, put it in analytics, or persist it unencrypted.
- Account export/delete task responses should use stable task ids and a narrow `status` enum such as `queued | in_progress | done | failed`.
- Feedback link must use `https://support.qq.com/product/{PRODUCT_ID}` with singular `product`; append only safe source context and do not pass phone, session, cookie, or raw user identifiers.
- Do not implement the full Epic 3.3 compliance backend in this story. KMS/Envelope helpers and queue entry points are enough for Epic 1 Settings entry; full object cleanup, telemetry anonymization, and Langfuse PII cleanup remain Epic 3.3.

### UX Guardrails

- Settings layout should be mobile-first single column with grouped sections: account/login, AI & BYOK, data/privacy, and feedback.
- BYOK copy from UX: save success "已保存"; validation failure "密钥无效，请检查"; platform fallback "已回退到平台额度".
- Feedback failure copy from UX: "页面无法内嵌，已在系统浏览器打开"; fallback form copy "内置表单已启用，可提交文本与截图".
- Account deletion requires an explicit confirmation interaction before calling `DELETE /account`.
- The Settings surface should feel like an operational product screen, not a marketing page; keep typography compact and controls scannable.

### Previous Story Intelligence

- Story 1.4 established `apps/mobile/src/home/` feature-folder patterns with focused API client and Testing Library coverage.
- Story 1.4 App tests stub `fetch` at the app boundary and verify session restore before Home; reuse that pattern for Settings navigation.
- Story 1.4 review added selected anchor source context and analytics sanitization for candidate internals. Settings analytics must continue that privacy posture for API keys and feedback text.
- Story 1.3/1.4 server probes use small Fastify apps with registered plugins/routes instead of booting the full production server. Use the same deterministic probe style for Settings contracts.

### Latest Technical Notes

- Use the repository-pinned frontend stack from `apps/mobile/package.json`: React 19.2.7, React DOM 19.2.7, Vite 8.0.16, Vitest 4.1.9, Testing Library React 16.3.2, and jsdom 29.1.1.
- Use the repository-pinned backend stack from `apps/server/package.json`: Fastify 5.8.5, Zod 3.23.8, Prisma Client 5.18.0, BullMQ 5.63.0, and TypeScript 5.4.5.
- Do not add dependencies for WebView in this web-based mobile MVP. Represent WebView open/fallback through a typed callback and testable UI state.

### Completion Notes List

- Ultimate context engine analysis completed - comprehensive developer guide created.
- Aligned Settings/BYOK/account/feedback OpenAPI contracts and regenerated generated API types.
- Extended existing BYOK routes to support `/user-key`, `/byok/validate`, `/byok/save`, and compatibility aliases without returning plaintext key material.
- Added authenticated feedback link route using the official `support.qq.com/product/{PRODUCT_ID}` URL pattern with safe source context.
- Implemented mobile Settings navigation from Home, Settings screen groups, BYOK save/failure/delete flows, account export/delete task display, and feedback fallback form.
- Extended privacy-safe analytics for Settings/account/feedback events and added focused mobile/server coverage.
- Addressed code review findings by retaining BYOK envelope data behind opaque key refs, aligning contracts, adding export confirmation/double-submit guards, adding quota-to-BYOK routing, and making feedback fallback submission/failure copy honest.
